featureName: BUG-021-006: bill/trip/return alert timing must be LLM-driven, not hardcoded N-day windows
reporter: owner directive (no const limits; LLM decides per situation)

executionHistory.summary: After BUG-021-005 made cooling LLM-driven, an audit of sibling hardcoded business thresholds found the bill/trip/return alert producers in the same alert_producers.go still decided 'alert now?' with hardcoded N-day windows: bill daysUntilBilling > 3; trip/return INTERVAL '5 days'. These answer the same domain question docs/smackerel.md §3.6 requires to be LLM-driven (alert timing depends on the event: large annual charge vs small monthly; international vs local trip; high-value vs trivial return).

Single bugfix-fastlane scope reusing the BUG-021-005 pattern: one alert_timing_evaluate scenario (alert_kind in input lets the LLM reason per-kind) + BridgeAlertTimingEvaluator (mockable) + a shared evaluateAndCreateTimedAlert helper; all three producers retrieve candidates within an operational lookahead horizon and let the LLM judge each; operational bounds (lookahead, cap, floor) to fail-loud SST. SCN-021-TIMING-01..03 authored.

Created config/prompt_contracts/alert-timing-evaluate-v1.yaml. internal/intelligence/alert_timing.go: AlertKind, AlertTimingCandidate (internal fields json:"-"), AlertTimingDecision, AlertTimingEvaluator + BridgeAlertTimingEvaluator, AlertTimingConfig, alertTimingShouldSurface, evaluateAndCreateTimedAlert helper, noop_alert_timing init(). Engine gained an alertTiming field + SetAlertTimingConfig. Reworked ProduceBillAlerts / ProduceTripPrepAlerts / ProduceReturnWindowAlerts to retrieve candidates within $lookahead_days (parameterized + capped) and route each through the LLM, gating on the confidence floor; graceful skip when nil. Removed the > 3 and INTERVAL '5 days' windows. SST: intelligence.alert_timing.* (smackerel.yaml + config.sh + internal/config/alert_timing.go). Wiring: wireAlertTimingEvaluator + main.go call. Removed a dead billCand.freq field.

alert_timing_test.go: scripted runner proves parse, scenario routing, public-signal forwarding, internal-field (ArtifactID/AlertType/Priority) non-leak, all error paths + the pure gate. config/alert_timing_test.go: SST populate + fail-loud + range. scenario-lint registers alert_timing_evaluate (11 registered); go build ./... + vet green.

Full intelligence + config + scheduler + cmd/core + scenario-lint packages green. ./smackerel.sh config generate resolves the new keys into dev.env (config-validate OK). Existing alert_producers_test.go helper tests unaffected (date math unchanged).
One scenario + one evaluator + one shared evaluateAndCreateTimedAlert helper for all three producers; removed a dead struct field. No duplication across the three kinds.

Each producer collects candidates before any LLM call (no DB cursor held during Invoke); context cancellation honored mid-loop; the scenario carries operational LLM-call bounds. Nil-evaluator path degrades gracefully.

AlertTimingCandidate internal fields (ArtifactID/AlertType/Priority) are json:"-" and proven (by test) not to enter the LLM envelope; only kind + subject + day-count + a composed detail string are sent. Candidate queries are parameterized (no SQL injection; the return-window regex guard is preserved). No framework files touched.
artifact-lint PASSED for the packet; config generate + build + vet + affected packages green. Evidence in report.md.

No hardcoded alert-timing window remains (grep of 'daysUntilBilling > 3' and INTERVAL '5 days' in alert_producers.go = 0). No migration; generated env gitignored; no .github/bubbles changes; parent spec 021 planning artifacts untouched.

Scenario YAML, alert_timing.go, smackerel.yaml, and wiring document the LLM-driven judgment and the operational/business boundary. The packet records continuation of BUG-021-005 and notes commitment_overdue/meeting_brief as event-driven (not threshold-judged).

scopeProgress.title: LLM-driven alert timing for bill/trip/return producers

lockdownState.reason: bugfix-fastlane LLM-driven rework; no convergence loop required

resolvedFindings.summary: The bill/trip/return alert producers decided 'alert now?' with hardcoded N-day windows (> 3, INTERVAL '5 days'), contradicting docs/smackerel.md §3.6 (domain reasoning must be LLM-driven) and the owner directive.
resolvedFindings.resolution: Moved the alert-timing judgment to the alert_timing_evaluate LLM scenario via BridgeAlertTimingEvaluator; the producers retrieve candidates within an operational lookahead horizon only; operational bounds moved to fail-loud SST. No hardcoded window remains.
resolvedFindings.evidence: report.md Test/Validation Evidence; internal/intelligence/alert_timing.go; config/prompt_contracts/alert-timing-evaluate-v1.yaml; grep of hardcoded windows = 0.
